title: Substack vs Sendinblue (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack vs Sendinblue: pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs.
intro: Substack and Sendinblue serve very different needs in the email marketing space. Substack is built for writers and creators monetizing direct subscriber relationships, while Sendinblue (now Brevo) excels as an all-in-one CRM with transactional email and SMS capabilities. Understanding their core differences will help you choose the right platform for your specific use case.

comparison_table.tool_b: Transactional email, SMS, CRM, and multi-channel campaigns

comparison_table.tool_a: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, notes feed, recommendations
comparison_table.tool_b: SMS, WhatsApp, transactional API, chat, integrated CRM

choose_a_if: You're a writer or creator looking to monetize your newsletter directly
You want unlimited subscriber capacity with no technical restrictions
Building audience engagement and community interaction is your priority
choose_b_if: You need transactional email, SMS, and multi-channel marketing in one platform
You're running an ecommerce business or SaaS requiring advanced CRM features
You want better customer support and higher satisfaction ratings

faq.a: Neither is universally better—they serve different purposes. Substack excels for writers monetizing content, while Sendinblue is superior for businesses needing transactional email, SMS, and CRM features. Choose based on your primary use case.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Sendinblue?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper if you have no paid subscribers (completely free). However, Substack takes 10% revenue on paid subscriptions. Sendinblue's paid plans start at $25/month but have no revenue share, making it more cost-effective for high-revenue newsletters.

faq.q: Can I switch from Sendinblue to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your contact list from Sendinblue and import it into Substack. However, note that Substack is designed primarily for newsletters, not general email marketing, so some features may not transfer directly.
